Good morning, Ana. Let us start your stand-up. What did you do yesterday?
Yesterday, I tested the new checkout page, and I have found two bugs so far.
Nice. Have you written the reports yet?
I have already written one, and I am finishing the second one right now.
Good. Do you have any blockers today?
Yes, one blocker. I am waiting for the new build, so I am a little behind schedule.
Okay. I will follow up with the dev team and loop you in.
Thanks. One action item: can we sync up about the release this afternoon?

Hi, and welcome to your first daily stand-up. Let me explain how it works. Every morning at nine, the whole team meets for fifteen minutes. Each person answers three questions: what did you do yesterday, what are you doing today, and do you have any blockers? Keep it short. This is a quick recap, not a long discussion. If something needs a longer talk, we turn it into an action item and follow up after the meeting. One rule matters most: if you are behind schedule or blocked, say it early. A stand-up is not a test. It is how we stay on track and help each other. So give a clear, honest update, and we finish on time.

Hi team, it is Sofia with my update for today's stand-up. Yesterday, I finished the test cases for the login screen, and I have already sent them to the developers. So far, I have found three small bugs, and I have reported all of them. Today, I am running the payment tests. I have not finished them yet, but I am on track. I do have one blocker: I am still waiting for the new build, so I am a little behind on that task. Could someone please follow up with the dev team? One more thing, an action item: we still need to sync up about the release date. Let us wrap up that decision this week. Thanks, everyone.

First, at nine o'clock, the whole team meets for the fifteen-minute stand-up. Then Ana gives a quick recap of what she did yesterday. Next, she says what she is working on today and shares one blocker. After that, Marco turns the blocker into an action item and offers to follow up with the dev team. Finally, after the stand-up, the team syncs up on the release details.
